title: Who I work best with
summary: I'm most effective when the context matches how I work: strategy and execution in one place, clarity over buzzwords, and room to reduce complexity.
content:
• Product and engineering leaders who want a senior partner to structure the roadmap, challenge the architecture, and deliver—not just execute tickets.
  • Teams with legacy or tangled systems who want to reduce debt, clarify structure, and stabilize without a full rewrite.
  • Advanced WordPress organisations who want to move towards headless, modernize Gutenberg, and structure clean APIs, with a clear long-term vision.
  • Multi-brand or multi-market companies that need to manage complexity, consistency, and performance without adding unnecessary layers.
  • Small teams (2–4 devs) under pressure who need clear prioritization, solid technical standards, and a structured framework to ship cleanly.
  • Organisations that need a real bridge between business and tech, with someone who can adapt their language and keep everything aligned.
  • Startups and scale-ups who want a responsible technical voice, able to hold the line from strategy to delivery.
